name: SumUp
one_liner: Global fintech company providing card readers, POS systems, and financial services tools to help small businesses accept payments and manage their finances.

ambiguities: Total funding figures vary across sources: Sacra reports ~$3.56B (equity + debt), PitchBook reports $4.26B, Clay reports $3.677B, and Tracxn reports $997M (equity only); $3.56B used as best blended estimate from Sacra.
Founded year reported as 2011 by PitchBook and some older sources, but 2012 is the more widely cited year in press releases and the company's own materials.
Headcount varies across sources: Tracxn (4,103 as of Apr 2026), LeadIQ (3.8K as of Feb 2026), PitchBook (3,768), company self-reported 'over 3,000'; 3,800 used as mid-range estimate.
HQ is operationally London (Great Marlborough Street) per LeadIQ and multiple sources, though some databases list Wilmington, Delaware as a registered entity address.
IPO discussions reported as of Sept 2025 at potential $15B valuation but no public listing confirmed as of data cutoff.
Last round (May 2024) was a private credit/debt financing, not a traditional equity round; valuation post-money not publicly disclosed.
